question: What is agentic RAG and how does it differ from standard RAG?

claim_lines: Agentic RAG is a retrieval-augmented generation system where an agent can **decide how to retrieve**, **use tools**, **iterate**, and sometimes **reflect/verify** before answering, rather than doing one fixed retrieve-then-generate pass. Standard RAG is the simpler baseline: retrieve relevant context from an index at query time, then pass that context to the model for generation.

In practice, agentic RAG adds orchestration logic around retrieval: the system may plan sub-queries, decide whether retrieval is needed, call retrieval as a tool, and re-retrieve if the first pass is insufficient. That makes it better suited to multi-step or ambiguous questions, but also increases system complexity and the chance of orchestration failures.
Self-reflective variants go one step further by having the model critique its own draft or retrieved evidence to improve factuality.

- standard retrieve-then-generate workflow — covered: standard RAG retrieves context at runtime and then generates, per the baseline paper and LangChain docs: https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/rag

- planning or iterative retrieval — covered: agentic RAG can decide whether to retrieve, generate subqueries, and re-retrieve in loops: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/agentic-rag

- tool use — covered: retrieval is exposed as a tool in the LangChain agentic RAG examples: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/rag https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/agentic-rag
- reflection or verification — covered: Self-RAG explicitly adds self-reflection/critique to retrieval and generation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11511

- added complexity and failure modes — covered: the survey notes static workflows’ limits and the added orchestration burden; Self-RAG notes indiscriminate retrieval can hurt outputs: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11511
